flags.note: חֶסֶד — No English equivalent. Combines covenant loyalty, active kindness, and steadfast love. "Mercy" is too weak; "lovingkindness" is archaic; "steadfast love" misses the relational dimension.

flags.note: אֱלֹהִים — Grammatically plural, normally takes singular verbs when referring to Israel's God. Translating as "God" (singular) interprets the plural; "gods" would be technically accurate but theologically wrong in monotheistic contexts.

flags.note: אֱלֹהִים — Grammatically plural, normally takes singular verbs when referring to Israel's God. Translating as "God" (singular) interprets the plural; "gods" would be technically accurate but theologically wrong in monotheistic contexts.
חֶסֶד — No English equivalent. Combines covenant loyalty, active kindness, and steadfast love. "Mercy" is too weak; "lovingkindness" is archaic; "steadfast love" misses the relational dimension.
עוֹלָם — "eternal" / "everlasting" / "age-long." Like Greek αἰώνιος, debate centers on whether this is absolute timelessness or the span of an era.
